_id in ElasticSearch is used to uniquely identify a document. 
CloudCanal allows the user to specify the column at the source as the source of the _id value. If the user does not specify, the value of the primary key column at the source will be used as the value written to the peer _id by default (when multiple columns are selected as _id 
, the connector is "$" by default, and the parameter "pkSeparator" can be modified in the personal center). 
In the scenario of using CloudCanal's custom code to build a wide table for multi-table join, it is recommended to set _id as the associated column of the source-side multi-table.

If the value is high, the bottleneck is on the target side.

project-flow-check-always: Confirmation is required in the SQL review phase before proceeding to subsequent stages.

project-flow-check-failure: Confirmation is required when the risk level in SQL review results is "Failure" to proceed to subsequent stages.
project-flow-check-skip: Skip SQL review and automatically proceed to subsequent stages.
project-flow-approve-enable: Enable process-associated approval. Proceed to subsequent stages after approval is granted.
project-flow-approve-disable: Disable process approval. Proceed to subsequent stages after SQL review passes.
project-flow-execute-auto: Automatically execute published SQL statements for changes.
project-flow-execute-manual: Require confirmation before executing published SQL statements for changes.
project-flow-execute-disabled: Only track and record SQL operations without performing any database actions.
project-flow-transactional-enable: Use transactions during change execution. CloudDM will attempt to rollback changes if failures occur (some database engines do not support rollback when DDL statements are present).
project-flow-error-none: Pause subsequent execution when encountering errors.
project-flow-error-retry: Attempt retries on execution errors. Pause subsequent execution if retries fail.
project-flow-error-skip: Skip erroneous statements and continue subsequent execution when encountering errors (use with caution).
gitops-init-snapshot: Build snapshots of the SQL scripts in the newly released stream, and be able to identify incremental changes when subsequent changes occur.
gitops-init-change: Create a new change and execute the existing SQL script by publishing the change.
